rationale: An LLM capable only of solving trivial input validation/DoS vulnerabilities (capability ceiling well below what OC3 ransomware operations require) provides minimal uplift for the technically demanding components of this attack chain: complex n-day exploitation of enterprise web applications, Active Directory privilege escalation, EDR evasion, and living-off-the-land lateral movement all exceed this LLM's capabilities. The marginal value is limited to basic reconnaissance automation and rudimentary scripting assistance (~10-15% barrier reduction on non-critical path tasks), suggesting only modest increase from the baseline of 20 actor groups. The expected multiplier of ~1.15x reflects that the binding constraints for OC3-level ransomware operations (operational infrastructure, RaaS relationships, existing technical tradecraft, criminal network access) are entirely unaddressed by this low-capability LLM.
